primary_question.question: Among all 50 US states and DC in 2021, was implementation of a statewide healthcare worker COVID-19 vaccine mandate (`Test-out option` and timing from hcw_mandates_table.csv), compared with no mandate, associated with lower COVID-19 mortality rates per 100,000 population (derived variable `covid_mortality_rate` from `Archive Link` downloads and `Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2021`)?
primary_question.population: All 50 US states and DC in July–December 2021, classified as mandate-adopting (n=16, from hcw_mandates_table.csv) versus non-adopting (n=35+, states absent from hcw_mandates_table.csv)
primary_question.exposure_or_intervention: Presence of a statewide healthcare worker COVID-19 vaccine mandate with or without `Test-out option` as documented in hcw_mandates_table.csv; primary binary contrast is mandate vs no mandate
primary_question.comparator: US states without a statewide healthcare worker vaccine mandate in 2021 (states absent from hcw_mandates_table.csv)
primary_question.outcome: State-level COVID-19 mortality rate per 100,000 population (derived variable `covid_mortality_rate`), computed from downloaded CDC archive data via `Archive Link` divided by `Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2021`
primary_question.study_design: Ecological cross-sectional study with state as the unit of analysis and mandate status as the binary exposure
primary_question.rationale: Healthcare worker vaccine mandates were a consequential pandemic policy intervention in 2021; their association with population-level COVID-19 mortality is directly relevant to JAMA Network Open readers and policymakers. State-level COVID-19 outcome data are obtainable via Archive Link URLs in the repository file. The NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx provides 2021 population denominators aligned with the mandate period.

secondary_questions.question: Among the 16 mandate-adopting states, did states offering a `Test-out option` differ from those requiring mandatory vaccination in the timing (`Date of announcement`) or type of facilities covered (`Main scope`)?
secondary_questions.analysis_type: Descriptive comparison with Mann-Whitney U test for timing and Fisher exact test for scope categorization; internal to the 16-state mandate subsample
secondary_questions.rationale: Characterizes whether test-out provisions reflected earlier political compromise or different implementation contexts, providing policy-relevant detail on mandate design variation.

secondary_questions.question: Among mandate-adopting states, was the breadth of facilities covered (`Main scope`, operationalized as derived `scope_breadth`) associated with the availability of a `Test-out option`?
secondary_questions.analysis_type: Spearman rank correlation between derived scope_breadth (count of facility types in Main scope string) and test-out indicator within the 16 mandate states
secondary_questions.rationale: Tests whether broader mandates covering more facility types were more likely to include test-out compromises, informing future mandate policy design.

data_acquisition_requirements.action: Read Archive Link column from United_States_COVID-19_Cases_and_Deaths_by_State_over_Time file. Select the snapshot whose Update Date is closest to 2022-01-01. Download the S3 .txt file at that URL. Parse tab- or comma-delimited content to extract state abbreviation, total_cases, and total_death for the period through December 2021. If the primary S3 URL is inaccessible, attempt the next-most-recent Archive Link. Join downloaded data with NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx on state name to compute covid_mortality_rate = total_death / Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2021 * 100000. Save the merged state-level file to the target path.

strengths: hcw_mandates_table.csv is complete for the 16 mandate-adopting states with zero missing values across all four columns, providing a clean policy exposure
Archive Link URLs in the COVID repository file provide a direct pathway to CDC-hosted COVID-19 outcome data for all 50 states, enabling mandate vs non-mandate ecological comparison
NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx contains July 2021 population estimates temporally aligned with the mandate period, enabling accurate per-capita rate computation
The 11:5 split on Test-out option is adequate for non-parametric testing within the mandate-adopting subgroup
limitations: Critical: N=16 mandate-adopting states is severely underpowered; the primary ecological comparison requires successfully downloading COVID outcome data from S3 Archive Link URLs that may be access-restricted or deprecated
Critical: All analyses are at the state (ecological) level; individual-level causal inference is not possible and the ecological fallacy applies
Unmeasured confounding: partisan environment, baseline vaccination rates, population density, median age, and healthcare capacity are not available in the provided datasets
Main scope has 11 distinct free-text values across only 16 states, requiring derivation of a summary numeric variable (scope_breadth) for analysis
Temporal misalignment risk: mandate announcement dates span July–August 2021 while COVID mortality accumulates over months; the window of impact attribution is uncertain
The COVID archive file contains metadata only; actual epidemiological outcomes require downloading from S3 URLs with no fallback source in the provided files
required_assumptions: Archive Link S3 URLs remain publicly accessible and accurately represent CDC-reported state-level COVID-19 case and death counts through December 2021
States absent from hcw_mandates_table.csv did not implement a statewide healthcare worker COVID-19 vaccine mandate in summer 2021
Geographic Area entries in NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx map to State abbreviations in hcw_mandates_table.csv via standard USPS abbreviation lookup; regional aggregate rows must be filtered out before joining
The 9.52% missing values in NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx are structurally missing due to regional aggregate rows (Northeast, Midwest, South, West, United States) that must be filtered out

excluded_variables.State: Identifier — used as join key between hcw_mandates_table.csv and population/COVID data; not an exposure or outcome
excluded_variables.Geographic Area: Identifier — geographic join key for population estimates in NST-EST2024-POP.xlsx; not an exposure or outcome
excluded_variables.April 1, 2020 Estimates Base: Population denominator — census base estimate superseded by July 2021 estimate aligned with the mandate period
excluded_variables.Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2020: Population denominator — pre-mandate period; not temporally aligned with 2021 analysis window
excluded_variables.Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2022: Population denominator — post-mandate period; not used in primary 2021 analysis
excluded_variables.Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2023: Population denominator — post-mandate period; not used in primary 2021 analysis
excluded_variables.Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2024: Population denominator — post-mandate period; not used in primary 2021 analysis
excluded_variables.Update Date: Archive repository metadata — records update timestamps of the CDC data repository software; not epidemiological data
excluded_variables.Days Since Update: Archive repository metadata — recency tracking of repository software updates; no epidemiological analytical value
excluded_variables.User: Archive repository metadata — constant single-value column (Tyler Service Account - HHS); no analytical information
excluded_variables.Metadata Published: Archive repository metadata — JSON metadata strings describing dataset schema; not analyzable epidemiological data
excluded_variables.Metadata Updates: Archive repository metadata — JSON records of schema update events; not analyzable epidemiological data
excluded_variables.Column Level Metadata: Archive repository metadata — constant empty-array column with single unique value; no analytical information
excluded_variables.Column Level Metadata Updates: Archive repository metadata — constant empty-array column with single unique value; no analytical information
excluded_variables.Archive Link: URL column — used as data acquisition source to download actual CDC COVID-19 state-level data files; the URL string itself is not an analytical variable

derived_variables.name: covid_mortality_rate
derived_variables.derivation: total_death (from downloaded CDC archive snapshot via Archive Link) / Population Estimate (as of July 1) 2021 * 100000. Units: deaths per 100,000 population.

derived_variables.name: mandate_status
derived_variables.derivation: Binary indicator: 1 if state appears in hcw_mandates_table.csv, 0 otherwise. Constructed by merging all 50 US states plus DC against the mandate table on state abbreviation/name.

derived_variables.name: scope_breadth
derived_variables.derivation: Integer count of distinct healthcare facility type tokens in the Main scope string. Parse by splitting on commas, semicolons, and 'and'; deduplicate; count. Range: 1 to approximately 6.
